creator: p5B2EA84B3@t-online.de
text:
1.) This needs to be exp-run again, possibly multiple times.
Can we have a look on the list of exp-runs, and the result of the last one.

2.) Comment 58
Did this happen? 

3.) Comment 60
What relevance does net/py-suds have, and what priority does this PR have?
Can we deprecate ports like this as they do not meet the new requirements?

4.) Comment 78
Is there a list of affected ports to work on?
Why is this really necessary at this stage?

5.) Comment 84
I do not see any relevance to the current default Python version.

Maybe such important issues should not be the burden of a single commiter, what it look like. 

As a matter of facts, if Python ports do need years for resolving problems with current upstream PIPs, there is a need to for reviewing/auditing the process Python gets handled by FreeBSD. Python does move faster than the cleanup here does happen.

For reference: https://reviews.freebsd.org/D46236
